summary.en: Take real money for a not-yet-built product — Kickstarter, pre-orders, deposits. The strongest desirability + viability signal you can get short of an actual product launch.
summary.zh: 为还没造出来的产品收真钱 —— Kickstarter、预购、定金。在产品真正发布之前,需求性 + 可营性的最强信号。

sources.label: Bland & Osterwalder · Testing Business Ideas · Wiley · 2019 · Validation / 4-23 Pre-Sales

headline.en: Real money before manufacturing at scale.
headline.zh: 规模化生产前，先收真实的钱。

story.en: Pebble tested demand for its smartwatch through a Kickstarter pre-sale before committing to manufacturing at scale. Backers did not merely click interest; they pledged money for a product that had not yet shipped, giving the team stronger viability evidence than signups or survey intent.
story.zh: Pebble 在投入规模化生产之前，通过 Kickstarter 预售测试智能手表需求。支持者不只是表达兴趣，而是为尚未交付的产品承诺付款，这比留邮箱或问卷意向提供了更强的可营性证据。

hypothesis.en: The Pebble team believed enough early adopters would pay upfront for a customizable smartwatch to justify manufacturing and fulfilment.
hypothesis.zh: Pebble 团队相信，会有足够早期用户愿意为一款可定制智能手表提前付款，从而支撑制造和履约投入。

experiment.en: They launched a Kickstarter campaign with a product story, demo materials, reward tiers, and delivery promises, using pledged dollars as the main demand signal.
experiment.zh: 他们发起 Kickstarter 活动，提供产品故事、演示材料、奖励档位和交付承诺，用承诺付款金额作为主要需求信号。

evidence.en: The campaign quickly exceeded its funding goal and became one of Kickstarter's breakout hardware pre-sales, proving strong early willingness to pay.
evidence.zh: 该活动很快超过融资目标，并成为 Kickstarter 早期标志性的硬件预售案例之一，证明了强烈的早期付费意愿。

insights.en: Pre-sales validate demand with stronger evidence, but also create fulfilment risk: every pledge becomes an operational promise the team must honor.
insights.zh: 预售用更强证据验证需求，但也制造履约风险：每一笔承诺付款都会变成团队必须兑现的运营承诺。

actions.en: Pebble used the funding and demand proof to move into production, while learning the operational difficulty of shipping hardware to a large backer base.
actions.zh: Pebble 用资金和需求证据推进生产，同时也学到向大量支持者交付硬件的运营难度。

examples.source: Bland & Osterwalder · Testing Business Ideas · Wiley · 2019 · Pre-Sales, pp. 282-283; Kickstarter · Pebble campaign · 2012

text.en:
Replace this with one fragile willingness-to-pay assumption.

e.g. [Target customers] need [outcome] badly enough to pay or place a deposit before the product is built.
text.zh:
替换成一条最脆弱的付费意愿假设。

例如：[目标客户] 对 [结果] 的需求强到愿意在产品做出来之前付款或付订金。

text.en:
We believe [target customers] will commit money for [offering] before it exists.

To verify, we will present a real pre-order / paid pilot offer to 50 qualified prospects within 3 weeks.

And measure paid conversions, deposit amount, refund requests, objections, and sales-cycle time.

We are right if ≥ 5 prospects pay at least [deposit/price] and refund requests stay below 10%.
text.zh:
我们相信 [目标客户] 会在 [提供物] 尚未存在时为它付钱承诺。

为了验证，我们将 在 3 周内向 50 位合格潜客提出真实预购 / 付费试点 offer。

并测量 付款转化、订金金额、退款请求、异议和销售周期。

如果 ≥ 5 位潜客支付至少 [订金 / 价格]，且退款请求 < 10% = 通过。

text.en:
Package: define exactly what buyers get, expected delivery date, refund policy, early-bird price, and capacity limit. Use a real checkout, invoice, or purchase order path.

Recruit: build a list of 50 qualified prospects from interviews, waitlist, communities, or sales network. Prioritize people with visible urgency and budget authority.

Sell: send a direct offer and run short calls. Do not accept “sounds interesting” as success; ask for card, deposit, signed order, or paid pilot. Track every objection in CRM.
text.zh:
打包：明确买方得到什么、预计交付日期、退款政策、早鸟价和容量上限。使用真实 checkout、发票或采购订单路径。

招募：从访谈、候补名单、社区或销售网络中整理 50 位合格潜客。优先选择紧迫度可见且有预算影响力的人。

销售：发出直接 offer 并做短通话。不要把「听起来不错」当成功；要求刷卡、订金、签单或付费试点。把每个异议记录到 CRM。

text.en:
Pass: ≥ 5 paid commitments at [price/deposit]; refund requests < 10%; at least 60% of payers match the intended segment.

Fail: prospects only join a free waitlist, ask for deep discounts, or payment requires features outside the core offer.

Inconclusive: strong verbal intent but purchasing friction blocks payment; test Letter of Intent with economic buyers.
text.zh:
通过：≥ 5 个按 [价格 / 订金] 付款承诺；退款请求 < 10%；付款者中至少 60% 属于目标客群。

失败：潜客只愿加入免费候补、要求大幅折扣，或付款依赖核心 offer 之外的功能。

不清晰：口头意愿强但采购摩擦阻止付款；改用 Letter of Intent 测经济买方。

text.en:
Fill in after running.

Prospects contacted: __ ; Sales calls: __ ; Paid commitments: __ ; Revenue/deposits: $__ ; Refund requests: __ .

Top objection: __ . Buyer profile of payers: __ . Verdict: validated / invalidated / inconclusive.
text.zh:
实验后填写。

触达潜客：__；销售通话：__；付款承诺：__；收入 / 订金：$__；退款请求：__。

最高频异议：__。付款者画像：__。结论：已验证 / 已证伪 / 不清晰。

text.en:
Pick one based on the result.

• Persevere → deliver the smallest promised version manually or via Wizard of Oz by the committed date.
• Pivot → adjust price, package, segment, or promise based on objections and payer profile.
• Kill → if people will not pay, do not substitute free waitlist interest for viability evidence.
text.zh:
根据结果选一个。

• Persevere → 在承诺日期前，用人工或 Wizard of Oz 交付最小承诺版本。
• Pivot → 根据异议和付款者画像调整价格、包装、客群或承诺。
• Kill → 如果人们不愿付款，不要用免费候补兴趣替代可营性证据。
